disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bilateral cold-triggered triphasic Raynaud's (white->blue->red) with reperfusion pain, plus mild symmetric arthralgia/synovitis
trigger_question_examples: What colour changes did your fingers go through, and both hands?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Both hands went white, then blue, now red as blood returns; my wrists and knees ache too.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No single digit staying persistently white/numb despite warming; no black/necrotic tip (critical digital ischaemia screen)
trigger_question_examples: Is any single finger staying white and numb even after warming?
Any finger tip turning dark blue/black or breaking down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — the colour is coming back to all fingers with warming, and no tip is black.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bilateral, symmetric, cold-triggered episode resolving with warming (against a single cold pulseless limb of embolism)
trigger_question_examples: Is it both hands equally and cold-triggered, or one persistently cold/pulseless limb?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Both hands equally, cold-triggered, and warming brings the colour back — not one cold dead limb.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No skin thickening/tight facial skin/telangiectasia or ulcers (systemic sclerosis progression screen)
trigger_question_examples: Any skin tightening on the fingers/face, or spider blood vessels?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No skin tightening/tight face/spider veins, and no finger ulcers.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Living alone in a cold house; unable to use hands for food/medication (self-care/warming risk)
trigger_question_examples: Can you warm up and use your hands, and is anyone helping?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My house is cold, I can't use my hands to cook or open medication, and I'm on my own.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Triphasic Raynaud's with recovering sluggish capillary refill; strong radial pulses; mild non-deforming wrist/knee synovitis; no ulcers/gangrene (examination)
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: (a finger-colour/rewarming self-check is possible; pulses/perfusion and joints need in-person assessment)

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Could I lose a finger, or is this just my Raynaud's? Do I need an ambulance?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Screen critical/irreversible ischaemia (a single digit staying white/numb despite warming, or a black/necrotic tip) and features of embolism (a single persistently cold, pulseless limb). If any are present, advise ED/000. If both hands are recovering with warming and there is no black tip, reassure it is a Raynaud's attack — advise gentle rewarming, warmth/gloves and prompt review — rather than an ambulance, with a clear critical-ischaemia safety-net. Do not dismiss the ischaemia question on the UCTD label.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: What can I do right now and for my joints?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Advise gentle rewarming (warm — not hot — water), warming the home, gloves, and continuing/adding a simple analgesic/NSAID for the arthralgia (with the usual cautions), while arranging GP/rheumatology review for a calcium-channel blocker and DMARD consideration. Do not present unproven agents as therapy.

deflection_behaviours.description: Patient fixates on losing a finger/ambulance, testing whether the AI Doctor screens critical ischaemia/embolism and responds proportionately with rewarming + prompt review.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: I'm sure I'll lose a finger, shouldn't I go by ambulance?
